volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 1.1.159 - Section 2.2.240b)

sections.label: Section 1.1.159
sections.original: Menedeme , at porro recte spero , et illum tibi
sections.translation: But still , Menedemus , I hope for the best , and

sections.label: Section 1.1.160
sections.original: Salvum affuturum esse hic confido propediem .
sections.translation: I trust that he ’ ll be here safe before long .

sections.label: Section 1.1.161
sections.translation: Oh that the Gods would grant it !

sections.label: Section 1.1.161b
sections.translation: They will do so . Now , if it is convenient to you — the festival of Bacchus [*] is being kept here to-day — I wish you to give me your company .

sections.label: Section 1.1.163
sections.translation: I can not .

sections.label: Section 1.1.163b
sections.translation: Why not ? Do , pray , spare yourself a little while . Your absent son would wish you do so .

sections.label: Section 1.1.165
sections.original: Non convenit qui illum ad laborem impulerim
sections.translation: It is not right that I , who have driven him hence to endure hardships , should now shun them myself .

sections.label: Section 1.1.166b
sections.translation: Is such your determination ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.167
sections.translation: It is .

sections.label: Section 1.1.167b
sections.translation: Then kindly fare you wall .

sections.label: Section 1.1.167c
sections.translation: And you the same .

sections.label: Section 1.1.167d
sections.translation: He has forced tears from me , and I do pity him . But as the day is far gone , I must remind Phania , this neighbor of mine ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.170
sections.original: Ad coenam ut veniat . Ibo , visam si domi est .
sections.translation: to come to dinner . I ’ ll go see whether he is at home . ( Goes to PHANIA ’ s door , makes the inquiry , and returns .) There was no occasion for me to remind him : they tell me he has been some time already at my house ; it ’ s I myself am making my guests wait . I ’ ll go in-doors immediately . But what means the noise at the door of my house ? I wonder who ’ s coming out ! I ’ ll step aside here .

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: Nihil adhuc est quod vereare , Clinia : haudquaquam etiam cessant :
sections.translation: There is nothing , Clinia , for you to fear as yet : they have not been long by any means : and I am sure that she will be with you presently along with the messenger . Do at once dismiss these causeless apprehensions which are tormenting you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.178
sections.translation: Who is my son talking to ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.179
sections.original: Pater adest quem volui : adibo . Pater , opportune advenis .
sections.translation: Here comes my father , whom I wished to see : I ’ ll accost him . Father , you have met me opportunely .

sections.label: Section 1.2.180
sections.translation: What is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.180b
sections.translation: Do you know this neighbor of ours , Menedemus ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.180c
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 1.2.181
sections.translation: Do you know that he has a son ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.181b
sections.translation: I have heard that he has ; in Asia .

sections.label: Section 1.2.181c
sections.translation: He is not in Asia , father ; he is at : our house .

sections.label: Section 1.2.182b
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.182c
sections.translation: Upon his arrival , after he had just landed from the ship , I immediately brought him to dine with us ; for from our very childhood upward I have always been on intimate terms with him .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184b
sections.translation: You announce to me a great pleasure .

sections.label: Section 1.2.185
sections.original: Quam vellem Menedemum invitatum ut nobiscum esset hodie amplius ;
sections.translation: How much I wish that Menedemus had accepted my invitation to make one of us : that at my house I might have been the first to surprise him , when not expecting it , with this delight !— and even yet there ’ s time enough —

sections.label: Section 1.2.187b
sections.original: Cave faxis : non opus est , pater .
sections.translation: Take care what you do ; there is no necessity , father , for doing so .

sections.label: Section 1.2.188
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.188b
sections.original: Quia enim incertum est etiam , quid se faciat . Modo venit ;
sections.translation: Why , because he is as yet undetermined what to do with himself . He is but just arrived . He fears every thing ; his father ’ s displeasure , and how his mistress may be disposed toward him .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190
sections.original: Eam misere amat : propter eam haec turba atque abitio evenit .
sections.translation: He loves her to distraction : on her account , this trouble and going abroad took place .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190b
sections.translation: I know it .

sections.label: Section 1.2.191
sections.original: Nunc servulum ad eam in urbem misit , et ego nostrum una Syrum .
sections.translation: He has just sent a servant into the city to her , and I ordered our Syrus to go with him .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192
sections.translation: What does Clinia say ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.192b
sections.translation: What does he say ? That he is wretched .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192c
sections.translation: Wretched ? Whom could we less suppose so ? What is there wanting for him to enjoy every thing that among men , in fact , are esteemed as blessings ? Parents , a country in prosperity , friends , family , relations , riches ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.195
sections.original: Atque haec perinde sunt ut illius animus qui ea possidet :
sections.translation: And yet , all these are just according to the disposition of him who possesses them . To him who knows how to use them , they are blessings ; to him who does not use them rightly , they are evils .

sections.label: Section 1.2.197
sections.original: Immo ille fuit senex importunus semper : et nunc nihil magis
sections.translation: Aye , but lie always was a morose old man ; and now I dread nothing more , father , than that in his displeasure he ’ ll be doing something to him more than is justifiable .

sections.label: Section 1.2.199
sections.original: Illene ? sed reprimam me : nam in metu esse hunc illi est utile .
sections.translation: What , he ? ( Aside .) But I ’ ll restrain myself ; for that the other one should be in fear of his father is of service to him . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.2.200
sections.translation: What is it you are saying to yourself ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.200b
sections.original: Dicam : ut ut erat , mansum tamen oportuit .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . However the case stood , Clinia ought still to have remained at home . Perhaps his father was a little stricter than he liked : he should have put up with it . For whom ought he to bear with , if he would not bear with his own father ? Was it reasonable that he should live after his son ’ s humor , or his son after his ? And as to charging him with harshness , it is not the fact . For the severities of fathers

sections.label: Section 1.2.205
sections.original: Uniusmodi sunt ferme , paulo qui est homo tolerabilis .
sections.translation: are generally of one character , those I mean who are in some degree reasonable men . [*] They do not wish their sons to be always wenching ; they do not wish them to be always carousing ; they give a limited allowance ; and yet all this tends to virtuous conduct . But when the mind , Clitipho , has once enslaved itself by vicious appetites , it must of necessity follow similar pursuits . This

sections.label: Section 1.2.210
sections.original: Scitum est ; periculum ex aliis facere , tibi quod ex usu siet .
sections.translation: is a wise maxim , to take warning from others of what may be to your own advantage .

sections.label: Section 1.2.211
sections.translation: I believe so .

sections.label: Section 1.2.211b
sections.original: Ego ibo hinc intro , ut videam nobis quid coenae siet .
sections.translation: I ’ ll now go hence in-doors , to see what we have for dinner . Do you , seeing what is the time of day , mind and take care not to be any where out of the way .

sections.label: Section 2.1.213
sections.original: Quam iniqui sunt patres in omnes adolescentes iudices ,
sections.translation: What partial judges are all fathers in regard to all of us young men , in thinking it reasonable for us to become old men all at once from boys ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.215
sections.original: Neque illarum affines esse rerum quas fert adolescentia .
sections.translation: and not to participate in those things which youth is naturally inclined to . They regulate us by their own desires ,— such as they now are ,— not as they once were . If ever I have a son , he certainly shall find in me an indulgent father . For the means both of knowing and of pardoning [*] his faults shall be found by me ; not like mine , who by means of another person , discloses to me his own sentiments .

sections.label: Section 2.1.220
sections.original: Perii ! is mihi , ubi adbibit plus paulo , sua quae narrat facinora !
sections.translation: I ’ m plagued to death ,— when he drinks a little more than usual , what pranks of his own he does relate to me ! Now he says , Take warning from others of what may be to your advantage . How shrewd ! He certainly does not know how deaf I am at the moment when he ’ s telling his stories . Just now , the words of my mistress make more impression upon me . Give me this , and bring me that , she cries ; I have nothing to say to her in answer , and no one is there more wretched than myself .

sections.label: Section 2.1.225
sections.original: Nam hic Clinia , etsi is quoque suarum rerum satagit , attamen
sections.translation: But this Clinia , although he , as well , has cares enough of his own , still has a mistress of virtuous and modest breeding , and a stranger to the arts of a courtesan . Mine is a craving , saucy , haughty , extravagant creature , full of lofty airs . Then all that I have to give her is — fair words [*] — for I make it a point not to tell her that I have nothing . This misfortune I met with not long since , nor does my father as yet know any thing of the matter .

sections.label: Section 2.2.230
sections.original: Si mihi secundae res de amore meo essent , iamdudum , scio ,
sections.translation: If my love-affairs had been prosperous for me , I am sure she would have been here by this ; but I ’ m afraid that the damsel has been led astray here in my absence . Many things combine to strengthen this opinion in my mind ; opportunity , the place , her age , a worthless mother , under whose control she is , with whom nothing but gain is precious .

sections.label: Section 2.2.234b
sections.translation: Clinia !

sections.label: Section 2.2.234c
sections.translation: Alas ! wretched me !

sections.label: Section 2.2.235
sections.original: Etiam caves ne videat forte hinc te a patre aliquis exiens ?
sections.translation: Do , pray , take care that no one coming out of your father ’ s house sees you here by accident .

sections.label: Section 2.2.236
sections.original: Faciam : sed nescio quid profecto mihi animus praesagit mali .
sections.translation: I will do so ; but really my mind presages I know not what misfortune .

sections.label: Section 2.2.237
sections.original: Pergin istuc prius diiudicare quam scis quid veri siet ?
sections.translation: Do you persist in making up your mind upon that , before you know what is the fact ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.238
sections.original: Si nihil mali esset , iam hic adessent .
sections.translation: Had no misfortune happened , she would have been here by this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.238b
sections.translation: She ’ ll be here presently .

sections.label: Section 2.2.238c
sections.translation: When will that presently be ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.239
sections.original: Non cogitas hinc longule esse ? et nosti mores mulierum :
sections.translation: You don ’ t consider that it is a great way fiom here . [*] Besides , you know the ways of women ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.240
sections.original: Dum moliuntur , dum conantur , annus est .
sections.translation: while they are bestirring themselves , and while they are making preparations a whole year passes by .

sections.label: Section 2.2.240b
sections.translation: O Clitipho , I ’ m afraid —
